passes_skipped_reasons.4: no openspec/specs/ baseline (greenfield change); no archived requirements to check against

findings.title: "one-screen" not defined; could split implementations
findings.recommendation: Either define "one-screen" (e.g., "≤24 visible lines on an 80×24 terminal") or explicitly mark it approximate ("no hard line limit; default view fits a typical terminal"). As written, two implementers could produce notably different default views.

findings.title: --limit N: behavior with N=0 or negative N unspecified
findings.recommendation: Add a scenario covering the edge cases (e.g., "WHEN --limit 0 THEN recent[] is empty"; "WHEN --limit -1 THEN orbit-status exits non-zero with an error").

findings.title: "most recent JSON" ambiguous — by which timestamp?
findings.recommendation: Specify the source explicitly. Recommend: "by the ISO-8601 timestamp embedded in the filename (e.g., review-proposal-2026-05-18T01-47-44Z.json), descending." This matches the bootstrap-openspec-orbit precedent and avoids mtime-vs-content-ts ambiguity.

findings.title: Tier-2 rule 4 — "task edit" definition ambiguous (file mtime vs git commit?)
findings.recommendation: Define explicitly. Recommend: "the most recent mtime of tasks.md". This is the simplest deterministic source and matches the rest of the spec's filesystem-based signals.

findings.title: Edge case unspecified: both explore.md AND changes/<name>/ exist (mid-promotion state)
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit precedence rule at the top of the inference list: "If openspec/changes/<name>/ exists, ignore openspec/explore/<name>/ for that name (changes/ wins)." Otherwise phase inference falls through to rule 5 (exploring) when changes/ has artifacts.

findings.title: Error paths not specified (missing openspec/, missing jq, corrupted .orbit-runs JSON)
findings.recommendation: Add a small "error handling" requirement to orbit-status-output spec covering: openspec/ not found in cwd or ancestors → non-zero exit + stderr; jq missing (already covered by task 2.5) → non-zero exit + stderr; malformed JSON in .orbit-runs/ → log warning to stderr and skip that file rather than failing the whole run.

findings.title: Multi-change tie-break on equal last_touched mtimes unspecified
findings.recommendation: Add a parenthetical: "on tie, lexicographic order of change name." Edge case but worth nailing down for determinism.

findings.title: next_unchecked extraction logic underspecified for malformed tasks.md lines
findings.recommendation: Note in design.md (or add a brief requirement) that next_unchecked is the description of the first line matching the regex `^- \[ \] [0-9]+\.[0-9]+ (.+)$`; malformed lines are skipped.

findings.title: 76 tasks across 18 groups — consider chunking note for apply
findings.recommendation: Add a brief preamble to tasks.md (or design.md) suggesting which task groups form logical apply chunks (e.g., groups 1–3 = skill scaffolding + project detection; 4–8 = data layer; 9–13 = recommendation + output + degradation; 14–17 = surface integration + docs; 18 = manual validation). Lets /opsx:apply pause at chunk boundaries for review.

findings.title: design.md "Open Questions" still has 3 unresolved items at proposal time
findings.recommendation: Decide whether to resolve before /opsx:apply or accept as deferred. The three items (jq portability cliff, stale_review mtime granularity, --all flag) all have reasonable defaults; consider locking them in design now or explicitly noting they're deferred-to-apply.

final_assessment: No critical issues. 6 warning(s) to consider. Ready to apply (with noted improvements).
